```python
def _format_duration(seconds: int) -> str:
    if seconds < 60:
        return f"{seconds}s"
    minutes = seconds // 60
    remaining_seconds = seconds % 60
    if minutes < 60:
        return f"{minutes}m {remaining_seconds}s" if remaining_seconds else f"{minutes}m"
    hours = minutes // 60
    remaining_minutes = minutes % 60
    return f"{hours}h {remaining_minutes}m"
```

1	import logging
2	from textwrap import dedent
3	from typing import Annotated
4	
5	from prompt_toolkit import PromptSession
6	from rich.console import Console
7	from rich.markdown import Markdown
8	
9	from tim import Project, ChangeBuilder
10	from tim.agent import Agent
11	from tim.codechange import apply_code_change
12	from tim.tools import view_file, run, ls
13	
14	_console = Console()
15	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
16	
17	STYLE_STATUS = "yellow"
18	STYLE_LLM = "cyan"
19	STYLE_ERROR = "red"
20	
21	
22	def _print_llm(text: str) -> None:
23	    if text is None:
24	        _console.print("No final message from the LLM, this is likely a bug.", style=STYLE_ERROR)
25	        return
26	    _console.print(Markdown(text), style=STYLE_LLM)
27	
28	
29	def code_change(
30	    project: Project,
31	    title: Annotated[str, "A brief title for this change"],
32	    plan: Annotated[
33	        str,
34	        "Your detailed plan for this change, including approach to take, filenames to modify and commands to run. You MUST provide as much detail as possible.",
35	    ],
36	) -> str:
37	    """
38	    Required for all coding tasks: creating new files, editing existing code, fixing bugs, adding features.
39	
40	    You MUST instruct this tool as you would a junior engineer.
41	    You MUST provide full details of the change along with your COMPLETE plan.
42	    """
43	
44	    _console.print(f"*** Starting coding agent for: {title} ***", style=STYLE_STATUS)
45	    _console.print(Markdown(plan), style=STYLE_LLM)
46	    _console.print("\nApproval steps:", style=STYLE_STATUS)
47	
48	    complete_when = ApprovalConditionsAgent(project, f"--- {title} ---\n{plan}").answer()
49	    change = (
50	        ChangeBuilder(title)
51	        .desc(plan)
52	        .shoulds(
53	            [
54	                "Use good, teutonic variable names",
55	                "No single letter variable names, outside of loop indexes or comprehensions",
56	                "Functions should be short, no larger than 20 lines",
57	                "Avoid duplicating code by extracting shared logic to reusable functions",
58	                "Minimise code inside an except block (extract to another function if neccessary)",
59	                "Follow the same patterns and conventions as the rest of the codebase",
60	            ]
61	        )
62	        .musts(
63	            [
64	                "Never silently swallow errors",
65	                "Avoid fallbacks like os.getenv('SOME_KEY', 'default-value'), fail early with os.environ['SOME_KEY']",
66	                "No docstrings or comments, the implementation must explain itself with good naming and composition",
67	                "No imports inside of functions",
68	            ]
69	        )
70	        .approvals(
71	            [
72	                "./lint passes",
73	                *complete_when,
74	            ]
75	        )
76	        .build()
77	    )
78	    for condition in complete_when:
79	        _console.print(f" - {condition}", style=STYLE_LLM)
80	    print()
81	    _console.print("Coding agent response:", style=STYLE_STATUS)
82	    response = apply_code_change(project, change)
83	    _print_llm(response)
84	    print()
85	    return response
86	
87	
88	class CliAgent(Agent):
89	    PROMPT = dedent(
90	        """
91	        You are assisting a software engineer, use the tools provided and your own knowledge to help
92	        in any way you can.
93	
94	        The engineer may refer to existing code or system behavior. Use tools to understand the current
95	        state if it will help the answer.
96	
97	        You MUST use the code_change tool when changing or adding project code.
98	        - This tool ensures edits conform to coding standards and guidelines. Changes made without this tool will be rejected.
99	        - You MUST give the code_change tool your full, researched plan.
100	
101	        Project root is: {root}
102	        {listing}
103	        """
104	    ).strip()
105	
106	    def __init__(self, project: Project):
107	        prompt = self.PROMPT.format(
108	            root=project.run("pwd").stdout.strip(),
109	            listing=project.run("ls -la").stdout,
110	        )
111	        super().__init__(
112	            project=project,
113	            system_prompt=prompt,
114	            tools=[code_change, view_file, run, ls],
115	        )
116	
117	    def run_forever(self):
118	        session = PromptSession()
119	
120	        while True:
121	            user_message = ""
122	            while len(user_message) == 0:
123	                user_message = session.prompt("> ").strip()
124	
125	            if user_message in ("/quit", "/q"):
126	                break
127	
128	            elif user_message == "/think":
129	                self.enable_reasoning = True
130	                _console.print("Thinking enabled", style=STYLE_STATUS)
131	                continue
132	
133	            elif user_message == "/nothink":
134	                self.enable_reasoning = False
135	                _console.print("Thinking disabled", style=STYLE_STATUS)
136	                continue
137	
138	            elif user_message == "/code":
139	                plan = self.messages[-1].content
140	                title = ExtractTitleAgent(project=self.project, plan=plan).answer()

301	def _extract_metadata(entries: list[dict], log_path: Path) -> LogMetadata:
302	    timestamps = [_parse_timestamp(entry["at"]) for entry in entries]
303	    agent = entries[0]["source"]["agent"]
304	    start_time = _format_timestamp(min(timestamps))
305	    end_time = _format_timestamp(max(timestamps))
306	    filename = log_path.name
307	
308	    return LogMetadata(
309	        filename=filename,
310	        agent=agent,
311	        entry_count=len(entries),
312	        start_time=start_time,
313	        end_time=end_time,
314	    )
315	
316	
317	def _parse_log_entries(raw_entries: list[dict]) -> list[LogEntry]:
318	    return [_parse_log_entry(raw) for raw in raw_entries]
319	
320	
321	def html_from_log(log_path: str) -> str:
322	    log_file_path = Path(log_path)
323	    raw_entries = _read_log_file(log_file_path)
324	    metadata = _extract_metadata(raw_entries, log_file_path)
325	    entries = _parse_log_entries(raw_entries)
326	    agent_colors = _collect_agents(entries)
327

336	        entry_html = _render_entry(entry, agent_colors, duration_seconds)
337	        html_parts.append(entry_html)
338	        previous_entry = entry
339	
340	    html_parts.append(_build_html_footer())
341	
342	    return "\n".join(html_parts)

"""Test cases for the _format_duration function to verify 'X seconds' format."""
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '.')
from tim.logviewer import _format_duration


def test_seconds_format():
    """Test that seconds are formatted as 'X second(s)'."""
    assert _format_duration(0) == "0 seconds", f"Expected '0 seconds', got '{_format_duration(0)}'"
    assert _format_duration(1) == "1 second", f"Expected '1 second', got '{_format_duration(1)}'"
    assert _format_duration(5) == "5 seconds", f"Expected '5 seconds', got '{_format_duration(5)}'"
    assert _format_duration(59) == "59 seconds", f"Expected '59 seconds', got '{_format_duration(59)}'"


def test_minutes_format():
    """Test that minutes are formatted as 'X minute(s)' with optional seconds."""
    assert _format_duration(60) == "1 minute", f"Expected '1 minute', got '{_format_duration(60)}'"
    assert _format_duration(61) == "1 minute 01 seconds", f"Expected '1 minute 01 seconds', got '{_format_duration(61)}'"
    assert _format_duration(120) == "2 minutes", f"Expected '2 minutes', got '{_format_duration(120)}'"
    assert _format_duration(125) == "2 minutes 05 seconds", f"Expected '2 minutes 05 seconds', got '{_format_duration(125)}'"


def test_hours_format():
    """Test that hours are formatted as 'X hour(s)' with optional minutes."""
    assert _format_duration(3600) == "1 hour", f"Expected '1 hour', got '{_format_duration(3600)}'"
    assert _format_duration(3661) == "1 hour 01 minutes", f"Expected '1 hour 01 minutes', got '{_format_duration(3661)}'"
    assert _format_duration(7200) == "2 hours", f"Expected '2 hours', got '{_format_duration(7200)}'"
    assert _format_duration(3600 * 24) == "24 hours", f"Expected '24 hours', got '{_format_duration(3600 * 24)}'"


def test_no_compact_format():
    """Ensure the compact format ('Xs', 'Xm', 'Xh') is NOT used."""
    assert 's"' not in _format_duration(5) + " dummy", "Should not contain compact 's' format"
    assert _format_duration(5) != "5s", "Should not use compact '5s' format"
    assert _format_duration(60) != "1m", "Should not use compact '1m' format"
    assert _format_duration(3600) != "1h00m", "Should not use compact '1h00m' format"

        print("✓ test_no_compact_format passed")

78	def _format_duration(seconds: int) -> str:
79	    if seconds < 60:
80	        return f"{seconds}s"
81	    minutes = seconds // 60
82	    remaining_seconds = seconds % 60
83	    if minutes < 60:
84	        return f"{minutes}m{remaining_seconds:02d}s" if remaining_seconds else f"{minutes}m"
85	    hours = minutes // 60
86	    remaining_minutes = minutes % 60
87	    return f"{hours}h{remaining_minutes:02d}m"

The rule "Never silently swallow errors" means that when handling exceptions or potential errors, we should not simply ignore them. We should either:
1. Log the error
2. Raise a new exception
3. Return an error indicator
4. Handle the error in a way that makes it visible

Let me look at the `_format_duration` function to see if there are any potential errors that could be silently swallowed:

```python
def _format_duration(seconds: int) -> str:
    if seconds < 60:
        return f"{seconds} second" if seconds == 1 else f"{seconds} seconds"

    total_minutes = seconds // 60
    remaining_seconds = seconds % 60
    if total_minutes < 60:
        minutes_label = "minute" if total_minutes == 1 else "minutes"
        if remaining_seconds:
            return f"{total_minutes} {minutes_label} {remaining_seconds:02d} seconds"
        return f"{total_minutes} {minutes_label}"

    total_hours = total_minutes // 60
    remaining_minutes = total_minutes % 60
    hours_label = "hour" if total_hours == 1 else "hours"
    if remaining_minutes:
        return f"{total_hours} {hours_label} {remaining_minutes:02d} minutes"
    return f"{total_hours} {hours_label}"
```

This function looks safe. It doesn't involve any file I/O, network calls, or operations that could fail. It's purely arithmetic and string formatting.

```python

```

However, this isn't a silent error—negative values would simply display as "-X seconds" or similar, which would immediately flag the problem. There's no exception handling or error suppression happening here.

```python
def _format_duration(seconds: int) -> str:
    if seconds < 60:
        return f"{seconds} second" if seconds == 1 else f"{seconds} seconds"

    total_minutes = seconds // 60
    remaining_seconds = seconds % 60
    if total_minutes < 60:
        minutes_label = "minute" if total_minutes == 1 else "minutes"
        if remaining_seconds:
            return f"{total_minutes} {minutes_label} {remaining_seconds:02d} seconds"
        return f"{total_minutes} {minutes_label}"

    total_hours = total_minutes // 60
    remaining_minutes = total_minutes % 60
    hours_label = "hour" if total_hours == 1 else "hours"
    if remaining_minutes:
        return f"{total_hours} {hours_label} {remaining_minutes:02d} minutes"
    return f"{total_hours} {hours_label}"
```

```python
def _format_duration(seconds: int) -> str:
    if seconds < 60:
        return f"{seconds} second" if seconds == 1 else f"{seconds} seconds"

    total_minutes = seconds // 60
    remaining_seconds = remaining_seconds = seconds % 60
    if total_minutes < 60:
        minutes_label = "minute" if total_minutes == 1 else "minutes"
        if remaining_seconds:
            return f"{total_minutes} {minutes_label} {remaining_seconds:02d} seconds"
        return f"{total_minutes} {minutes_label}"

    total_hours = total_minutes // 60
    remaining_minutes = total_minutes % 60
    hours_label = "hour" if total_hours == 1 else "hours"
    if remaining_minutes:
        return f"{total_hours} {hours_label} {remaining_minutes:02d} minutes"
    return f"{total_hours} {hours_label}"
```
